proton.servicetemplate.CfnAbout: Create a service template. The administrator creates a service template to define standardized infrastructure and an optional CI/CD service pipeline. Developers, in turn, select the service template from AWS Proton. If the selected service template includes a service pipeline definition, they provide a link to their source code repository. AWS Proton then deploys and manages the infrastructure defined by the selected service template. For more information, see [AWS Proton templates](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/proton/latest/userguide/ag-templates.html) in the *AWS Proton User Guide*.

proton.servicetemplate.Description.Description: A description of the service template.

proton.servicetemplate.DisplayName.Description: The service template name as displayed in the developer interface.

proton.servicetemplate.EncryptionKey.Description: The customer provided service template encryption key that's used to encrypt data.

proton.servicetemplate.Name.Description: The name of the service template.

proton.servicetemplate.PipelineProvision.Description: If `pipelineProvisioning` is `true`, a service pipeline is included in the service template. Otherwise, a service pipeline *isn't* included in the service template.

proton.servicetemplate.Tags.Description: An object that includes the template bundle S3 bucket path and name for the new version of a service template.
